author: @nigelwilliams7920
text: The gearbox will be a nice source of heat due to the friction losses!  In a perfect design the available output torque from the wind turbine will be matched to the torque load of the torque-to-electricity converter part of the system so that the maximum tip to wind speed ratio of the blade tips is not exceeded at design wind speed. In other words - the load (coil design, number of poles etc) should be matched directly to the turbine power curve without the need for gearing up.  But its a lovely wee gear box anyway in'it!?

author: @Rpol_404
text: I love it Robert.  We are currently working on an actuator at my employer that uses a very similar planetary gear system.  Not to state the obvious, a major factor we found was gear quality is paramount to efficiency.  Any gear “roughness” had a direct effect on the overall performance.  3D printing gears is excellent for proof of concept, but good quality gears will produce much better efficiency.

author: @BenEehayeh
text:
What is the point in designing a gearbox at the cost of lost torque?
Design the stator and rotor for the RPM's you need. A low RPM stator can supply energy at hand crank speeds, no need for a gearbox.

text:
The peak voltage only matters if your insulation breaks down.

You can always transform the power to a lower voltage and higher amperage, and the impedance is your resistor, only it wasted a lot less energy than resistive losses, especially if you use a ferite coil.

I’m still at a question why wind-turbine blades are so slim at the tips. I can understand bird/bat/aeroplane wings being so, for weight to lift and strength considerations. If weight re lift isn’t a factor, the spine/hub of the blades can be beefed up easily to cope.

replies.author: @danedmiston9673
replies.text:
A wind turbine needs a dedicated charge controller, preferably a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) controller, rated for the battery voltage of the solar generator if they are to be used together. 

-still waiting for THAT VIDEO too.  I expect at least a few year wait.

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text: @danedmiston9673 Can you outline some practical (or technical) principles of how a MPPT operates as an interface between a source of power/energy and storage?  Just asking.  Thanks.

replies.author: @danedmiston9673
replies.text:
@bikerfirefarter7280 For solar panels there is Rated Voltage, an Open Circuit (no load) Voltage, and a Voltage at Maximum Power (the voltage were the panel operates most efficiently).

For example a solar panel Rated at 12 volts may have an Open Circuit Voltage of about 21 volts and a Voltage at Maximum Power of about 14.3 volts in full sunlight.  As the sunlight dims the Open Circuit Voltage will drop as well as the Voltage at Maximum Power.  If the Open Circuit Voltage is measured in the reduced sunlight then the new Voltage at Maximum Power can be calculated.  For many solar panels the Voltage at Maximum power will be about 68% of the Open Circuit Voltage.

A simple charge controller only limits the voltage to the battery to protect the battery.  An MPPT will protect the battery but also tries to maintain the solar panel at its Voltage of Maximum Power.  A google search will show many different ways that can be used to constantly estimate the Voltage of Maximum Power and then maintain it. Some MPPT controllers will allow charging in very dim sunlight by boosting the voltage from the panel up to the required charging voltage.

For wind turbines the Voltage of Maximum Power is based on the RPM of the alternator.  A good wind turbine MPPT will allow for custom power curves to be programed into the controller.

Another thing to consider for wind turbines is that the efficiency of the wind turbine is tied to the turbine RPM as a ratio of the wind speed (TSR).  Unless the alternator and turbine are very well matched it is hard to achieve their full efficiency potential even with the best MPPT.

author: @dhavaljatiya6795
text:
Hi I am regular viewer of your channel I like the way you explain and do practical stuff.
 
I have a question....regarding wind turbine and power generation. 

Can we use piston instead of direct power generation from windmill to compressed air and use air engine or turbine to generate electricity at constant rpm?

I thought about it because vawt or hawt slow wind will not produce enough power to charge battery, but slow wind will compressed air (slowly) which can used it later to generate electricity. 

I don't know about efficiency and related number.....I just have a thought....

Regards

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure - you can think about the compressed air as a kind of battery - and it has lots of advantages over normal batteries

replies.author: @dhavaljatiya6795
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering thank you for your reply. I am going to make a darwin wind turbine but had a confusion regarding compressed air or direct power generation. 

If possible can you explain it's advantages you mentioned in comments in your future video?

One more thing I Don't know that how much torque it required for compressed air and should I have to use gear to increase torque? And how much it can compressed air in a tank in term of psi. Means can we expect 120 psi with a small piston in 200 ltr tank....😀

Sorry to annoy you but I have many questions and don't have answers.

author: @Nirdian
text: So I wonder, how much wattage can this produce? Sure there can be around 100 volts but what would be the current?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh we will get what we want mate and then some - I modeled this after a best selling folding wind turbine which produces 40 watts from a 28mph wind - that's 12.5M per second - selling for $400 - we have already beaten that in lower wind speed tests

replies.author: @arkatub
replies.text: Current could be anything, it depends on the device being powered, a device "pulls" the amps from the generator, the amount "pulled" increases with voltage - an LED array rated at 25v 0.25A would draw 1A at 100v (it would also burn out). The same device can be built with more parrelel or series elements to work at different voltages.

replies.author: @onnonius
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering Imagine how much power you could have bought with that amount of investment. I don't think you will ever get to a profit. Just like the big ones, that only run subsidized. Sorry that I don't believe in it but keep the joy in experimenting. Love your video's btw.

replies.author: @SinsBird
replies.text: @onnonius you can make profit by building and selling the turbine

replies.author: @DFPercush
replies.text: How much power it produces depends on how much wind power goes into it. How big is the swept area and wind speed. The question is, how efficient is it at capturing that power.

author: @uscitizen5656
text: Looks like there's quite a bit of resistance to turn and with light wind it may never move. Need to see your entire system to evaluate the estimated output. Waiting with baited breath because I need to make this, to charge my hydroponic batteries if the sun don't shine on the panels. Thanks Rob!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a problem for all wind turbines mate - it's why they have a start wind speed as they need a minimum torque to get them turning

replies.author: @Struthio_Camelus
replies.text:
How much energy is there in low velocity wind, and is that juice worth the squeeze?

Intermittent generation is the bugaboo with most small-scale generation. Sometimes the sun doesn't shine, sometimes the wind doesn't blow. 
Current thought is to store energy generated in times of plenty to use in times of scarcity.

replies.author: @mikejones-vd3fg
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkeringRight, maybe the solution is to put the fan blades on the planetary gears themselves at each level, so when its light wind it activates the low gear and then slowly builds up, if thats even possible?  Anyways thats what i was imagining when seen those staacking gears, it remined me of an automatic transmision, but for wind.  That would be cool if it would work, a turbine that works at all  wind speed, maybe these planetary gears or  magnetic ones are the key to do it.

author: @AETERNACOMMAND
text:
Hey Robert and community,
I develop a handbook on how to integrate automatic systems into nature, so that every human can live free and in best health from the fruits of this earth (paradise).

In my region (central europe) it is to cold to sleep outside without shelter. To counter this circumstance, a telluric current driven floor heating system would be great, and I think it would a perfect project for the channel.

I thought that a 50cm x 150cm closed rectangular water pipe, which is slightly angled in height at one pipe corner, in order to allow water flow through a water boiling resonator attached to the exterior pipe - would be a possible solution, which can be dug into the ground (soil).

It would be great if you or someone from the community would come up with such an automatic system which I (and everybody) can build efficiently from scavenged resources.

Best regards,
Quentin

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate
